description: Curated slicer & filter recipes. Strategist binds every row of Design Spec §10 (Filters & Interactions) to one of these. Use slicer-filter-patterns.md for decision logic; use these recipe files for composition, ASCII mockups, and property snippets.

recipes.title: Chiclet / Tile Slicer (Low-Cardinality Buttons)
use_when: 2–7 values always visible upfront (Channel, Segment, Region group)
Touch-first or TV-wallboard surfaces
Primary navigation-like filter on executive pages
anti_use: More than 7 values — tiles wrap and lose scanability
Long labels (> 12 chars) on narrow canvas

recipes.title: Dropdown Slicer with Search
use_when: 8–25 values; user knows the name they want
Secondary filters that shouldn't dominate canvas
Left-rail global filter panel
anti_use: ≤ 7 values (use chiclet tiles — always-visible is better)
> 10 000 values (use search-box pattern or drillthrough)

recipes.title: Hierarchy Slicer (Expand / Collapse)
use_when: Nested dimension (Year → Quarter → Month; Region → Country → City; Product Category → Subcategory → SKU)
Users drill at their own pace
Single slicer replaces 3 cascading slicers
anti_use: Flat dimensions (use dropdown)
Users always pick at the leaf — go straight to SKU search

recipes.title: Date Between Slider (Absolute Range)
use_when: User needs to pick an arbitrary start and end date
Retrospective / investigation reports
Ad-hoc analytical exploration
anti_use: Report reviewed on a regular cadence (use relative date — auto-advances)
Mobile — slider thumbs are hard to hit on touch

recipes.title: Relative-Date Rolling (Last N / Current)
use_when: Report reviewed daily, weekly, or monthly — needs to auto-advance
Operational ops pages (Last 7 days)
Executive monthly reviews (Last complete month / Current quarter)
anti_use: Retrospective pages locked to a fixed period (use locked absolute filter)
Year-over-year comparison where both periods must stay visible

recipes.title: Numeric Range Slider
use_when: Bounding a numeric measure (Order value, Age, Tenure)
Histogram or distribution analysis pages
Outlier / anomaly exploration
anti_use: Discrete numeric codes that are really categorical (use dropdown)
When exact value matters (use input box / parameter)

recipes.title: Search-Box Lookup (Very High Cardinality)
recipes.control_type: slicer (dropdown, search-only) OR text search visual
use_when: Customer ID, SKU, Invoice ID — user types what they know
Detail/lookup pages
Replacing a slicer that would render > 10 000 items
anti_use: User doesn't know the exact value (use drillthrough from a summary visual)
≤ 25 values (use dropdown)

recipes.title: Left-Rail Global Slicer Panel
use_when: 3–5 global filters shared across an Analysis page group
Wide desktop canvas (16:9, 1280×720)
Analyst audience comfortable with a fixed sidebar
anti_use: Executive single-page dashboards (use top header bar)
Mobile portrait (rail eats too much width — use collapsed filter pane)
Only 1 filter (slicer doesn't justify a rail)

recipes.title: Top-Header Compact Filter Bar
use_when: 2–4 primary filters on an executive or operational page
TV wallboards (filters visible on display, rarely changed)
Mobile landscape
anti_use: ≥ 5 filters (rail is better)
When filters need long category labels

recipes.title: Sync-Slicer Group (Narrative Continuity)
use_when: Overview + 2–3 Analysis pages tell one narrative
User expects filter state to persist when navigating
Mobile parity with desktop
anti_use: Drillthrough target pages (they receive their own context)
Tooltip pages
Pages serving different audiences / mental models

recipes.title: Hidden & Applied Filter (Governance)
use_when: Data-quality hygiene filters (`[IsActive] = TRUE`, `[IsTestRecord] = FALSE`)
Tenant / region pre-scoping that shouldn't appear as a slicer
Page pinned to a fixed period (e.g. 'Q1 Retrospective')
anti_use: Filters users should be able to change (use slicer)
Hiding filters to disguise incomplete data — always visible in audit

recipes.title: Role-Based Bookmark Filter Toggle
use_when: All-access users (controllers, directors) need to switch between 'My scope' and 'All scope'
RLS removes a dimension but 1–2 roles still need it
Executive audience wants pre-canned view switches without touching filters
anti_use: Every user sees the same scope (use a plain slicer)
≥ 5 view variants (use a slicer or field parameter instead — bookmarks become unmaintainable)

recipes.title: Field Parameter Measure/Dimension Switch
use_when: User wants to switch the measure shown on a chart (Revenue / Units / Margin)
User wants to switch the dimension on an axis (By Region / By Channel / By Brand)
Reducing visual count by letting one chart serve 3–5 views
anti_use: Audiences that won't notice / use the toggle (adds complexity)
Measures with incompatible formats on the same axis (mix $ and %)

recipes.title: What-If Parameter Slicer
use_when: Scenario pages (Price elasticity, FX sensitivity, Discount %)
Goal seek / target-setting workflows
Letting users drive a calculation input without editing the model
anti_use: Filtering existing rows (use a normal slicer)
When the parameter must persist across sessions (use an Excel-connected parameter table instead)
